industry: Customer Relationship Management / Enterprise SaaS / Agentic AI
thesis: Salesforce is the #1 CRM platform and is converting its franchise to agentic AI, with Agentforce ARR of $1.2B (+205% YoY) and combined AI + Data ARR of $3.4B, while total revenue still grows double digits and RPO stands at $67.9B. The stock has de-rated ~40% from its high on fears that AI agents erode seat-based SaaS pricing, leaving it at ~11.8x FY27 non-GAAP EPS guidance with a ~10% trailing FCF yield. Management is leaning against the narrative with a debt-funded $25B accelerated buyback (~12% of shares retired) and guided H2 FY27 organic revenue acceleration. The disruption fear is priced as if growth stalls; reported numbers show the opposite, skewing risk/reward positively.

drivers: Agentforce becomes a proven consumption revenue engine: AI + Data ARR ($3.4B, tripling YoY) keeps compounding and Agentic Work Units (+111% Q/Q) convert into billable usage at scale
Guided H2 FY27 organic revenue acceleration materializes, breaking the 'AI kills SaaS' narrative and re-rating the multiple from ~12x toward 18–20x forward non-GAAP EPS
$25B ASR plus remaining $50B authorization shrink share count ~12%+, mechanically compounding EPS on top of margin expansion (non-GAAP OM 34.3% guided)
Informatica cross-sell strengthens Data 360 / Zero Copy positioning as the data foundation for enterprise agents
FY30 targets regain credibility, putting $16–18 of non-GAAP EPS power in view by FY28

assumptions: Revenue lands within FY27 guidance of $45.9–46.2B (+11%, ~3pts from Informatica) with organic growth holding in the 8–9% range
Non-GAAP operating margin at the guided 34.3% and non-GAAP EPS at $14.06–$14.12
Agentforce keeps scaling but consumption revenue stays a single-digit percentage of total, so the market pays ~14–15x forward EPS rather than a full re-rate
Buybacks offset AI-capex and interest drag; FCF grows ~4–5% as guided
No visible seat erosion in Sales/Service Cloud renewal cohorts

risks: Agentic AI starts visibly cannibalizing seat counts at renewal, pushing organic growth to mid-single digits and cRPO growth below 10%
The $25B ASR debt turns from accretive to constraining as interest expense caps FCF growth while the multiple compresses to 9–10x
Agentforce ARR growth decelerates sharply as pilots fail to convert to production at scale
Microsoft (Copilot/Dynamics) and native-AI challengers win agent workloads on price, forcing discounting
Informatica integration distracts and dilutes margins

catalysts.event: Q2 FY27 earnings — guide is $11.27–11.35B (+10–11%); watch cRPO growth (~14% guided) and Agentforce ARR trajectory
Final settlement of the $25B accelerated share repurchase (~20% of ASR shares still to be delivered)
Dreamforce 2026 — Agentforce roadmap, enterprise production case studies, potential FY30 target updates
Q3 FY27 earnings — key checkpoint for the guided H2 organic revenue acceleration
Q4 / full-year FY27 results and initial FY28 guidance
Agentforce / Data 360 ARR disclosures and consumption-pricing traction (Agentic Work Units +111% Q/Q, 28.6T tokens processed)

key_risks: Agentic AI could structurally erode per-seat SaaS pricing across Sales and Service Cloud — the core bear thesis behind the ~46% one-year drawdown
$25B of new debt issued to fund the ASR reduces balance-sheet flexibility and caps FY27 FCF growth at ~4–5%
Organic growth ex-Informatica is ~8%; macro softness or seat rationalization could push it to mid-single digits
Informatica ($8B acquisition) integration and margin-dilution risk
Intense competition from Microsoft Copilot/Dynamics, ServiceNow, HubSpot, and AI-native startups
Stock-based compensation (~$3.6B/yr) keeps GAAP-vs-non-GAAP gap wide, though the shrinking share count now more than offsets dilution
